title: About
text:
sagui is a photometric counterpart to capivara. The package keeps the general IFU segmentation logic, but it shifts the focus from representative median spectra to region-integrated photometric SEDs with uncertainty propagation.
This makes it useful for workflows where segmentation is only the first step, followed by:

SED fitting
region-wise flux integration
stellar population analysis
property-map generation

title: About
text:
COIN Toolbox
sagui is part of the broader COIN Toolbox effort to provide open, reusable tools for astronomical inference, statistical modeling, and data-driven workflows.
The COIN identity is community-oriented, method-focused, and designed to support reproducible science.

title: About
text:
Design Principles

concise public API
robust defaults for real IFU cubes
clear separation between segmentation and region-level summaries
optional acceleration without forcing heavyweight dependencies

title: sagui
text:
Overview
sagui is a photometric SED-based segmentation package for IFU data cubes. It is designed to identify spatial regions using a photometric representation of the cube, apply a starlet-based mask, cluster valid spaxels into regions, and extract integrated SEDs with propagated uncertainties for each region.
In contrast to a purely spectral segmentation workflow, sagui is centered on:

photometric preconditioning of the cube
multiscale masking through starlet reconstruction
segmentation of valid regions
integrated region SEDs and uncertainties
region-level property maps for downstream SED fitting


Part of the COIN Toolbox, with a focus on concise, community-oriented tools for astronomical data analysis.

title: sagui
text:
Core Workflow
The intended workflow is:

collapse the IFU cube into a photometric summary image
build a starlet mask from the collapsed image
segment the masked cube into regions
extract integrated SEDs and uncertainties for each region
project derived region properties back onto the image plane

The main user-facing functions are:

segment_regions()
build_starlet_mask()
extract_region_sed()
summarize_region_sed()
plot_region_map()
plot_region_property_map()
plot_region_property_raster()

title: sagui
text:
Installation
Install from GitHub:
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("RafaelSdeSouza/sagui")
library(sagui)
If you want the faster clustering backend, also install the optional acceleration packages:
install.packages("fastcluster")
install.packages("torch")

title: sagui
text:
Usage

Segment an IFU Cube
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(sagui)
  library(FITSio)
})

x <- FITSio::readFITS("manga-8135-12701-LOGCUBE.fits")

seg <- segment_regions(
  input = x,
  Ncomp = 30,
  use_starlet_mask = TRUE,
  starlet_J = 5,
  starlet_scales = 2:5
)

plot_region_map(seg, palette = "magma")


Extract Integrated SEDs
sed <- extract_region_sed(
  cube = x,
  labels = seg$cluster_map,
  band_values = FITSio::axVec(3, x$axDat)
)

head(sed$flux_long)


Plot a Region Property
plot_region_property_map(
  seg,
  values = setNames(sed$flux_wide$n_pix, sed$flux_wide$region),
  value_label = "Pixels per region"
)

title: sagui
text:
Package Logic
The segmentation stage and the SED summary stage are intentionally separated:

clustering operates on scaled per-spaxel vectors
region summaries are based on integrated SEDs, not median representative spectra

This makes sagui better aligned with workflows where the final product is a region-integrated SED plus uncertainty for fitting or physical interpretation.

title: sagui
text:
Dependencies

FITSio for FITS cube handling
dplyr, tidyr, tibble for table reshaping
ggplot2, viridis, sf for visualization
matrixStats and Matrix for efficient numerical operations
fastcluster and torch as optional clustering accelerators

title: sagui
text:
References

Bundy, Kevin, et al. (2015). Overview of the SDSS-IV MaNGA Survey: Mapping Nearby Galaxies at Apache Point Observatory. The Astrophysical Journal, 798(1), 7. https://doi.org/10.1088/0004-637X/798/1/7
de Souza, R. S., et al. (2025). CAPIVARA: A spectral-based segmentation method for IFU data cubes. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 539(4), 3166-3179. https://doi.org/10.1093/mnras/staf688
Starck, J.-L., Murtagh, F., and Fadili, J. (2015). Sparse Image and Signal Processing: Wavelets, Curvelets, Morphological Diversity. Cambridge University Press.
